task_completion.description: Create the widget layout file with required TextView IDs
task_completion.visual_evidence: Android Studio editor showing 'widget_weather.xml' containing TextView elements with android:id attributes for '@+id/widget_city_name', '@+id/widget_temperature', and '@+id/widget_condition'.

task_completion.description: Create the widget metadata configuration file with specific attributes
task_completion.visual_evidence: Android Studio editor showing 'weather_widget_info.xml' containing an <appwidget-provider> tag with attributes minWidth="180dp", minHeight="60dp", and updatePeriodMillis="1800000".

task_completion.description: Implement the Kotlin provider class extending AppWidgetProvider
task_completion.visual_evidence: Android Studio editor showing 'WeatherWidgetProvider.kt' where the class extends 'AppWidgetProvider' and overrides the 'onUpdate' method, including the usage of 'RemoteViews'.

task_completion.description: Register the widget receiver in AndroidManifest.xml
task_completion.visual_evidence: Android Studio editor showing 'AndroidManifest.xml' with a <receiver> tag for the widget provider. It must contain an <intent-filter> with action 'android.appwidget.action.APPWIDGET_UPDATE' and a <meta-data> tag referencing the widget info XML.

task_completion.description: The project builds successfully upon completion
task_completion.visual_evidence: Android Studio 'Build' tool window at the bottom showing a successful Gradle build (e.g., 'BUILD SUCCESSFUL in ...', 'Build: successful', or a green checkmark indicating the assembleDebug task completed without errors).

integrity.description: Agent used Android Studio to edit files and build the project
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Android Studio IDE interface being used for file editing and project management, rather than terminal commands like 'echo' or 'cat' to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: Build success is from an actual Gradle execution
integrity.visual_evidence: The build success message appears in the standard Android Studio Build/Run tool window with typical Gradle output logs, not as a mocked text file or a simple terminal printout.

integrity.description: Files are edited within the correct WeatherApp project structure
integrity.visual_evidence: The Android Studio Project explorer or editor tab tooltips show the files are located in the 'WeatherApp' project directory (e.g., app/src/main/res/...) and use the 'com.example.weatherapp' package.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is located at /home/ga/AndroidStudioProjects/WeatherApp with package com.example.weatherapp. The VLM should verify four specific files: 1) app/src/main/res/layout/widget_weather.xml containing IDs widget_city_name, widget_temperature, widget_condition. 2) app/src/main/res/xml/weather_widget_info.xml with minWidth=180dp, minHeight=60dp, updatePeriodMillis=1800000. 3) app/src/main/java/com/example/weatherapp/WeatherWidgetProvider.kt extending AppWidgetProvider. 4) app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml containing the receiver with APPWIDGET_UPDATE intent.
